channel.title: Protocol Labs R&D
channel.description: Driving Breakthroughs in Computing to Push Humanity Forward.

item.title: Allison Duettmann — Why We Don't Have to Die: Cryonics, Uploading, and Existential Hope
item.description: Juan Benet speaks with Allison Duettmann, CEO of the Foresight Institute, about why cryonics and mind emulation could mean we don't have to die, how whole-brain emulation and connectomics could keep humans compatible with superintelligent AI, how nanotechnology could rebuild the physical world, and

item.title: How PL R&D Accelerates Breakthroughs
item.description: The first in a series: how PL R&D moves frontier ideas from open research into deployed systems across four focus areas — how we drive, measure, and sustain breakthroughs, and what our track record teaches us.

item.title: Can Quadratic Funding Go Mainstream? Ma Earth, Hypercerts & the Future of Public Goods
item.description: Kevin Owocki sits down with Matthew Monahan (Ma Earth) and Holke Brammer (Hypercerts Foundation) to discuss a bold new experiment: a $1 million Quadratic Funding round designed to bring public goods funding beyond the crypto ecosystem and into the mainstream.

item.title: Securing Fundamental Rights in the Digital Realm: The Infrastructure Freedom Now Runs On
item.description: The digital foundations our fundamental rights now depend on.

item.title: Konrad Kording — The Power of a Single Neuron and a Path to Simulating the Brain
item.description: Juan Benet speaks with Konrad Kording of the University of Pennsylvania about the surprising computational power of a single neuron, reading the brain's wiring down to molecular detail, and building compilers and simulations as a path toward understanding — and ultimately simulating — the brain.

item.title: Tom Oxley — Reading the Brain Without Opening the Skull
item.description: Juan Benet speaks with Tom Oxley, co-founder and CEO of Synchron, about the Stentrode — a brain-computer interface that reaches the motor cortex through a blood vessel without opening the skull — restoring independence to people with motor impairment and the path toward higher-channel-count neural i

item.title: Jacques Carolan — The Mission to Get Breakthrough Brain Treatments to Everyone
item.description: Juan Benet speaks with Jacques Carolan, a founding Programme Director at ARIA, about getting breakthrough brain treatments to everyone, how ambitious science programs can accelerate neurotechnology, and what it takes to move hard neurotech ideas toward patient impact.

item.title: Ben Rapoport — Treating Paralysis and Digitizing Brain Data
item.description: Juan Benet speaks with Ben Rapoport of Precision Neuroscience about treating paralysis, building scalable brain-computer-interface platforms, and how high-quality neural data can transform both clinical care and computational neuroscience.

item.title: How to Obtain a Complete Human Connectome at Synaptic Resolution Within the Next Decade
item.description: A PL Neuro whitepaper outlining what it would take to obtain a complete human connectome at synaptic resolution within the next decade, including the technical and organizational bottlenecks facing large-scale human brain mapping.

item.title: Max Hodak — Restoring Sight, Growing Neurons on Silicon, and Expanding Human Intelligence
item.description: Juan Benet speaks with Max Hodak of Science Corporation about restoring sight with retinal implants, biohybrid neural interfaces that grow neurons on silicon, the Science BCI ecosystem, and paths toward expanding human intelligence.

item.title: Announcing the Juan Benet Podcast: Launching With First Guest Max Hodak, CEO of Science Corp
item.description: Juan Benet Podcast launches with Max Hodak on neurotech, brain interfaces, Fast R&D, and more.

item.title: Models to Markets: BCI, Neural Data, and Business Innovation in the AI Era
item.description: The first issue in Representations, a limited series produced in partnership with PL Neuro, exploring how BCI companies are becoming neural-data and AI companies. It surveys emerging neuroAI business models, data strategy, clinical-commercial pathways, and the market dynamics that may expand neurote

item.title: Compiling Molecular Ultrastructure Into Neural Dynamics
item.description: A proposal for an ultrastructure-to-dynamics compiler: a learned mapping from molecularly annotated neural ultrastructure to simulator-ready, uncertainty-aware physiological parameters. The paper outlines the paired structure and perturbation-response data needed to make high-resolution brain maps p

item.title: Smoothing the Path to Clinical Revenue
item.description: Roadmap 1 in the Protocol Labs Brain–Computer Interface Roadmap Series, focused on the translational, regulatory, reimbursement, and business-model work needed to help BCI companies reach sustainable clinical revenue.

item.title: Overview: A Strategic Vision for the Future of Brain–Computer Interfaces
item.description: Roadmap 0 in the Protocol Labs Brain–Computer Interface Roadmap Series, setting out a strategic vision for the future of BCIs and the field-level milestones needed to move promising neural-interface research toward broad deployment.

item.title: Cognitive Dark Matter: Measuring What AI Misses
item.description: This paper argues that modern AI systems miss important training signals — cognitive dark matter — for domains such as metacognition, cognitive flexibility, episodic memory, lifelong learning, abductive reasoning, social reasoning, common-sense reasoning, and emotional intelligence. It proposes pair

item.title: Data Sovereignty in a Privacy-First Future
item.description: Builders redefining privacy and sovereignty — where control of data, identity, and computation is real, not just promised. Highlights from the teams working at the frontier of digital human rights.

item.title: Can Attestation Follow in the Footsteps of TLS?
item.description: Attestation could follow TLS's path: standardize trust signals, clarify roles, and mature tooling. A look at what it would take for remote attestation to become infrastructure-grade.

item.title: Universal Data Income: Paying Contributors on-Chain
item.description: AI was trained on us. How do we value data and pay the people who create it? David Dao, co-founder and Chief Scientist of GainForest, unpacks data valuation, Shapley values as a mechanism for attributing data contributions, and how a green data marketplace could fund conservation while improving AI

item.title: Threads From the 2025 Cypherpunk Retreat
item.description: Key takeaways from the Cypherpunk Retreat, where 100 leading builders advanced privacy-tech coordination. Themes included TEEs, MPC, and the next generation of private computation.

item.title: AI Safety and Governance: Hard Rules, Real Evaluations, and Risk
item.description: Evan Miyazono, founder and CEO of Atlas Computing, discusses how US and Chinese approaches to AI regulation are diverging and what that means for global AI safety. While the US moves toward a largely unregulated model, China requires mandatory evaluations for large-scale AI training runs. The conver

item.title: Quantum Computing and Bitcoin: The Security Threat No One's Talking About
item.description: Bolton Bailey, a researcher specializing in post-quantum cryptography, explains how Grover's algorithm could enable a quantum-powered miner to dominate the Bitcoin network, rewrite transaction history, and undermine its consensus mechanism — not by stealing private keys, but by breaking the proof-of

item.title: BitRobot and the Future of Embodied AI
item.description: Exploring how crypto incentives and decentralized infrastructure can accelerate embodied AI and robotics — and why open coordination matters for the next wave of physical AI.

item.title: Driving Breakthroughs: The Impact of Our Innovation Network
item.description: Reflecting on Protocol Labs' 2024 impact across neurotech, digital human rights, AI, and economies — and the vision for 2025 and beyond.

item.title: The Transformative Power of Neurotechnology
item.description: A deep dive into neurotechnology's potential to reshape treatment of brain disorders, augment human cognition, and accelerate our understanding of the brain. Why Protocol Labs is investing in this frontier.

item.title: PL Vision: Driving Breakthroughs in Computing to Push Humanity Forward
item.description: At a crossroads of technological capability and institutional inadequacy, Protocol Labs articulates its mission: building the infrastructure for a more open, equitable, and resilient internet.

item.title: A finality calculator for Filecoin’s Expected Consensus
Filecoin Proof of Useful Space
Security analysis of Filecoin's Expected Consensus in the Byzantine vs honest model
Base fee manipulation in Ethereum's EIP-1559 transaction fee mechanism
SpaceVDF: Verifiable delay functions using cryptographic satellites
LURK: Lambda, the ultimate recursive knowledge
tlock: Practical timelock encryption from threshold BLS
Generalized Impact Evaluators
Pikachu: Securing PoS blockchains from long-range attacks by checkpointing into Bitcoin PoW using Taproot
Enriching Kademlia by partitioning
To the InterPlanetary File System – and beyond!: Peer-to-peer file sharing would make the Internet far more efficient
Structure-preserving compilers from new notions of obfuscations
Mir-BFT: Scalable and robust BFT for decentralized networks
Decentralized hole punching
